text.tspan: "There is a question that we don't know the answer yet. Will it push customers to shift from on-prem to cloud?"
"The part that’s different is that companies that have traditionally said no to cloud are maybe more interested in taking a look and
"I think that as you see that there are vulnerabilities in hardware... as you see, again, supply chain shortages, especially around
memory right now, I do think all of those things are pushing more and more people to evaluate that they need at least some part of
the cloud as part of their infrastructure."
"We've been through shortages. We've been through macroeconomic headwinds. A couple of thoughts here … We are starting to
see some movement to from on-prem solutions to actually cloud. And we're starting to see that, and we expect that to happen going
"One of the interesting things that we see right now with the change in price and in supply on things like memory is that it is a
further impetus pushing companies who have on-premises infrastructure into the cloud."
"In terms of how we're reacting … Next I would say is the consideration of offloading things to cloud."
Commentary on Potential Migration to Cloud Due to Memory Cost Inflation
